Transformers use self-attention to weigh the importance of different input parts simultaneously.
Attention helps models focus on the most relevant parts of the input when generating output.

Gradient descent iteratively adjusts parameters to minimize the loss function.
Overfitting is when a model memorizes training data instead of learning general patterns.

RL trains agents through trial and error using rewards and punishments.
A loss function measures how wrong a model's predictions are during training.
Dropout randomly deactivates neurons during training to prevent over-reliance on specific paths.
Fine-tuning takes a pre-trained model and continues training on a specific task.
